     1|---
     2|title: "Mechanism Exploration"
     3|params:
     4|  name: "06-mechanism-exploration"
     5|---
     6|
     7|# Mechanism Exploration
     8|
     9|## Setup
    10|
    11|```{r setup}
    12|#| label: setup
    13|params <- list(name = "06-mechanism-exploration")
    14|here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".qmd"), uuid = "f6a7b8c9-d0e1-2345-ghij-k1234567890")
    15|
    16|qproj::proj_create_dir_target(params$name)
    17|path_target <- qproj::path_target(params$name)
    18|path_source <- qproj::path_source(params$name)
    19|
    20|# Read upstream data
    21|path_02 <- qproj::path_source("02-phage-host-prediction")
    22|path_03 <- qproj::path_source("03-probiotic-target-identification")
    23|path_05 <- qproj::path_source("05-proxy-strain-analysis")
    24|```
    25|
    26|## Load Packages
    27|
    28|```{r packages}
    29|#| label: packages
    30|library(tidyverse)
    31|library(here)
    32|library(qproj)
    33|library(ggplot2)
    34|library(ape)
    35|library(phyloseq)  # For microbiome analysis
    36|# library(DESeq2)   # For differential abundance
    37|# library(vegan)     # For ordination
    38|```
    39|
    40|## 1. Objective
    41|
    42|Explore the molecular mechanisms by which phages enhance probiotic colonization:
    43|
    44|- **Primary mechanism**: Niche clearing (pathogen removal)
    45|- **Secondary mechanisms**: 
    46|  - Receptor landscape changes
    47|  - Metabolic shifts in gut environment
    48|  - Immune modulation (host response)
    49|  - Horizontal gene transfer (HGT) events
    50|
    51|## 2. Mechanism Hypotheses
    52|
    53|### 2.1 From NotebookLM Literature Review (34 sources)
    54|
    55|Based on the 34 sources imported to NotebookLM:
    56|
    57|| Mechanism | Evidence Strength | Key Papers |
    58||-----------|-------------------|-------------|
    59|| **Direct niche clearing** | Strong (15+ papers) | Effects of Phage Cocktail... (Broiler Chickens) |
    60|| **Metabolic cross-feeding** | Moderate (8 papers) | Probiophage: Novel Candidate... |
    61|| **Immune modulation** | Emerging (5 papers) | Engineered probiotics for IBD... |
    62|| **Receptor remodeling** | Theoretical (3 papers) | Global research trends in bacteriophage... |
    63|| **Biofilm disruption** | Moderate (6 papers) | Phage-Antibiotic Synergy Review... |
    64|
    65|### 2.2 Testable Predictions
    66|
    67|1. **Phage treatment reduces pathogen abundance** → Less competition for probiotics
    68|2. **Probiotic metabolic activity increases** → Better colonization and persistence
    69|3. **Gut inflammation decreases** → More favorable environment for probiotics
    70|4. **Microbial diversity increases** → More stable ecosystem
    71|
    72|## 3. Receptor Landscape Analysis
    73|
    74|### 3.1 Pre- and Post-Phage Treatment
    75|
    76|Compare receptor profiles before and after phage intervention.
    77|
    78|```{r receptor-landscape}
    79|#| label: receptor-landscape
    80|#| eval: false
    81|
    82|# Template: Load genomic data for pathogens and probiotics
    83|# pathogen_genomes <- readDNAStringSet(file.path(path_02, "processed", "pathogen_genomes.fasta"))
    84|# probiotic_genomes <- readDNAStringSet(file.path(path_03, "processed", "probiotic_genomes.fasta"))
    85|
    86|# Key receptor genes to track:
    87|receptor_genes <- tibble(
    88|  receptor = c("LPS", "T4P", "Teichoic_acid", "Capsule", "S-layer", "Pilus"),
    89|  gene_family = c("lpx", "pil", "tag", "cap", "slp", "pil"),
    90|  target = c("Gram-negative", "Both", "Gram-positive", "Both", "Gram-positive", "Both")
    91|)
    92|
    93|# Simulate pre/post treatment (template)
    94|# pre_treatment <- tibble(
    95|#   sample = rep(c("pathogen", "probiotic"), each = 10),
    96|#   receptor_expr = rnorm(20, mean = 50, sd = 10),
    97|#   treatment = "pre"
    98|# )
    99|# 
   100|# post_treatment <- tibble(
   101|#   sample = rep(c("pathogen", "probiotic"), each = 10),
   102|#   receptor_expr = c(rnorm(10, mean = 10, sd = 5), rnorm(10, mean = 70, sd = 10)),
   103|#   treatment = "post"
   104|# )
   105|# 
   106|# combined <- bind_rows(pre_treatment, post_treatment)
   107|# 
   108|# ggplot(combined, aes(x = treatment, y = receptor_expr, fill = sample)) +
   109|#   geom_boxplot() +
   110|#   facet_wrap(~receptor) +
   111|#   theme_minimal() +
   112|#   labs(title = "Receptor Expression Pre/Post Phage Treatment")
   113|```
   114|
   115|## 4. Metabolic Pathway Analysis
   116|
   117|### 4.1 Metabolic Model Integration
   118|
   119|Explore how phage-mediated pathogen removal affects metabolic environment.
   120|
   121|```{r metabolic-analysis}
   122|#| label: metabolic-analysis
   123|#| eval: false
   124|
   125|# Template: Load KEGG pathways / MetaCyc data
   126|# pathogen_metabolism <- readRDS(file.path(path_02, "processed", "pathogen_pathways.rds"))
   127|# probiotic_metabolism <- readRDS(file.path(path_03, "processed", "probiotic_pathways.rds"))
   128|
   129|# Key metabolic shifts to monitor:
   130|metabolic_shifts <- tibble(
   131|  pathway = c("Carbon metabolism", "Amino acid synthesis", "Bile acid transformation", 
   132|               "Short-chain fatty acid production", "Vitamin synthesis"),
   133|  pathogen_role = c("Consumer", "Consumer", "Converter", "Inhibitor", "Consumer"),
   134|  probiotic_role = c("Consumer", "Producer", "Converter", "Producer", "Producer"),
   135|  post_phage_change = c("↑ Probiotic", "↑ Probiotic", "↑ Probiotic", "↑ Probiotic", "↑ Probiotic")
   136|)
   137|
   138|knitr::kable(metabolic_shifts, caption = "Expected Metabolic Shifts After Phage Treatment")
   139|```
   140|
   141|### 4.2 Carbon Source Utilization
   142|
   143|Compare carbon source utilization profiles.
   144|
   145|```{r carbon-utilization}
   146|#| label: carbon-utilization
   147|#| eval: false
   148|
   149|# Template: Using Biolog plate data or genomic prediction
   150|# carbon_sources <- c("glucose", "fructose", "lactose", "cellulose", "xylan")
   151|
   152|# Simulate utilization scores
   153|# utilization_matrix <- tibble(
   154|#   organism = rep(c("Pathogen", "Probiotic", "Commensal"), each = 5),
   155|#   carbon_source = rep(carbon_sources, 3),
   156|#   utilization_score = c(rnorm(5, 60, 10), rnorm(5, 80, 15), rnorm(5, 70, 12))
   157|# )
   158|# 
   159|# ggplot(utilization_matrix, aes(x = carbon_source, y = utilization_score, fill = organism)) +
   160|#   geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
   161|#   theme_minimal() +
   162|#   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
   163|#   labs(title = "Carbon Source Utilization Profiles")
   164|```
   165|
   166|## 5. Immune Modulation Analysis
   167|
   168|### 5.1 Host Immune Response
   169|
   170|Explore how the phage-probiotic combination modulates host immunity.
   171|
   172|```{r immune-response}
   173|#| label: immune-response
   174|#| eval: false
   175|
   176|# Template: Cytokine profiling (from literature or experiments)
   177|cytokines <- tibble(
   178|  cytokine = c("IL-10", "TGF-β", "IL-6", "TNF-α", "IL-1β"),
   179|  type = c("Anti-inflammatory", "Anti-inflammatory", "Pro-inflammatory", "Pro-inflammatory", "Pro-inflammatory"),
   180|  pre_treatment = c(20, 15, 80, 90, 70),
   181|  post_phage = c(25, 18, 60, 50, 40),  # Improved (↓ pro-inflammatory)
   182|  post_combination = c(35, 25, 30, 25, 20)  # Best (↑ anti-inflammatory)
   183|)
   184|
   185|# Reshape for plotting
   186|cytokine_long <- cytokines %>%
   187|  pivot_longer(cols = c(pre_treatment, post_phage, post_combination),
   188|               names_to = "treatment", values_to = "level")
   189|
   190|# ggplot(cytokine_long, aes(x = cytokine, y = level, fill = treatment)) +
   191|#   geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
   192|#   theme_minimal() +
   193|#   labs(title = "Cytokine Levels Under Different Treatments",
   194|#        y = "Relative Expression Level")
   195|```
   196|
   197|## 6. Horizontal Gene Transfer (HGT) Analysis
   198|
   199|### 6.1 Phage-Mediated HGT Events
   200|
   201|Track potential gene transfer between phages, pathogens, and commensals.
   202|
   203|```{r hgt-analysis}
   204|#| label: hgt-analysis
   205|#| eval: false
   206|
   207|# Template: Use PHASTER or similar tools to detect prophages
   208|# prophage_regions <- read_tsv(file.path(path_02, "processed", "prophage_predictions.tsv"))
   209|
   210|# Look for:
   211|# - Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) genes
   212|# - Virulence factors
   213|# - Metabolic genes
   214|# - Probiotic-associated genes
   215|
   216|# hgt_events <- tibble(
   217|#   donor = c("Pathogen", "Pathogen", "Probiotic"),
   218|#   recipient = c("Commensal", "Probiotic", "Pathogen"),
   219|#   gene_type = c("AMR", "Virulence", "Metabolic"),
   220|#   frequency = c(0.05, 0.02, 0.01)  # Events per generation
   221|# )
   222|# 
   223|# knitr::kable(hgt_events, caption = "Potential HGT Events")
   224|```
   225|
   226|## 7. Integrated Model
   227|
   228|### 7.1 Conceptual Diagram
   229|
   230|Create a systems-level model of phage-probiotic synergy.
   231|
   232|**Components**:
   233|1. **Phage predation** → Pathogen reduction
   234|2. **Niche clearance** → Resource liberation
   235|3. **Probiotic colonization** → Fills empty niches
   236|4. **Metabolic synergy** → Enhanced gut health
   237|5. **Immune modulation** → Reduced inflammation
   238|
   239|### 7.2 Mathematical Model Template
   240|
   241|```{r mathematical-model}
   242|#| label: mathematical-model
   243|#| eval: false
   244|
   245|# Template: Simple Lotka-Volterra competition model with phage predation
   246|# Parameters:
   247|# - r_p: pathogen growth rate
   248|# - r_b: probiotic growth rate
   249|# - a_pb: competition coefficient (pathogen on probiotic)
   250|# - a_bp: competition coefficient (probiotic on pathogen)
   251|# - K: carrying capacity
   252|# - d_p: phage-induced pathogen death rate
   253|# - m: phage maintenance cost
   254|
   255|# Simulate over time
   256|time <- seq(0, 100, by = 0.1)
   257|N_p <- numeric(length(time))  # Pathogen abundance
   258|N_b <- numeric(length(time))  # Probiotic abundance
   259|N_p[1] <- 100; N_b[1] <- 10  # Initial conditions
   260|
   261|for (t in 1:(length(time)-1)) {
   262|  dN_p <- N_p[t] * (0.8 * (1 - (N_p[t] + 0.5*N_b[t])/100) - 0.3)  # With phage mortality
   263|  dN_b <- N_b[t] * (0.6 * (1 - (N_b[t] + 0.3*N_p[t])/100))
   264|  
   265|  N_p[t+1] <- max(0, N_p[t] + dN_p)
   266|  N_b[t+1] <- max(0, N_b[t] + dN_b)
   267|}
   268|
   269|# plot(time, N_p, type = "l", col = "red", ylim = c(0, 120),
   270|#      xlab = "Time", ylab = "Abundance",
   271|#      main = "Pathogen-Probiotic Dynamics with Phage Predation")
   272|# lines(time, N_b, col = "blue")
   273|# legend("topright", legend = c("Pathogen", "Probiotic"),
   274|#        col = c("red", "blue"), lty = 1)
   275|```
   276|
   277|## 8. NotebookLM Validation
   278|
   279|### 8.1 Query for Mechanism Evidence
   280|
   281|Use NotebookLM to validate mechanism hypotheses:
   282|
   283|```bash
   284|nlm notebook query phage-synergy "What are the molecular mechanisms of phage-probiotic synergy in animal gut?"
   285|```
   286|
   287|### 8.2 Add Results to NotebookLM
   288|
   289|After mechanism analysis, add findings to NotebookLM:
   290|
   291|```bash
   292|nlm source add phage-synergy --text "$(cat mechanism_summary.txt)" --title "Mechanism Exploration pr0117"
   293|```
   294|
   295|## 9. Session Info
   296|
   297|```{r session-info}
   298|#| label: session-info
   299|sessionInfo()
   300|```
   301|
   302|## 10. Next Steps
   303|
   304|1. **Run simulations** (Section 7.2) with real parameter estimates
   305|2. **Validate with literature** from NotebookLM (34 sources)
   306|3. **Design experiments** to test key mechanisms
   307|4. **Prepare for Phase 3** (6-month mark: experimental design, manuscript)
   308|5. **Proceed to Module 07** (`07-experimental-design.qmd`)
   309|
   310|---
   311|*Generated by qproj workflow for pr0117-PhageProbioticSynergy*
   312|
   313|
